perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A girl in a white knit sweater and a black Kangol beret stands in a kitchen, holding a Labubu plush toy and looking surprised. (2) A hand is seen putting several red boxes into a refrigerator door. (3) A second girl with long black hair wearing a black off-the-shoulder top enters the kitchen, looks around suspiciously, and then opens the refrigerator to find the red boxes. (4) The two girls sit at a marble table unboxing the red 'The Monsters Vinyl Face' (Labubu x Coca-Cola) boxes. (5) They pull out Labubu plushies with different accessories, specifically small glass and canned Coca-Cola replicas. (6) They show off the toys to the camera, comparing different versions and checking the tags. (7) A series of close-ups shows the details of the Labubu toys holding the mini Coca-Cola items. (8) The girls pose together with the full set of toys.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, high-tempo pop-style BGM starts immediately. A female voice speaks excitedly in Mandarin with a high-pitched tone. (2) Sound effects of a refrigerator door opening and plastic/cardboard rustling occur. (3) A different female voice is heard, sounding questioning and then surprised. (4) Both females engage in a fast-paced dialogue with overlapping laughter and exclamations. (5) Frequent 'ding' and sparkle sound effects are added during product reveals. (6) Towards the end, a deeper male voice provides a rhythmic narration/voice-over while the music continues. (7) The video ends with a final musical flourish and the sound of the girls cheering.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: '姊姊偷把我的 Labubu 冰冰箱' (Sister secretly put my Labubu in the fridge). (2) Hook Text: '結果居然!?' (The result was!?). (3) Functional Text: '可樂 labubu' (Cola Labubu) - excited tone. (4) Functional Text: '隱藏隱藏 labubu' (Hidden version Labubu) - hopeful tone. (5) Descriptive Text: '玻璃可樂罐' (Glass coke bottle) and '罐裝可樂罐' (Canned coke) identifying versions. (6) Hook Text: '最後出奇跡' (A miracle at the end) - dramatic tone. (7) Decorative Text: '玻璃罐' (Glass bottle) with a star effect. (8) Brand/Slogan Text: '可口可樂 生活快樂' (Coca-Cola, happy life). (9) Decorative Text: '最貴的可樂' (The most expensive cola).

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Prank intro -> Discovery -> Joint unboxing -> Product showcase.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main female creators onscreen throughout the unboxing.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: One girl in a white knit sweater and black beret; one girl in a black ruffle-neck top with long dark hair.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Modern home interior featuring a high-end refrigerator, a marble kitchen island, and a dining area with plush toys visible in the background.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: This video serves as an engaging product demonstration and lifestyle vlog designed to promote the 'The Monsters Vinyl Face' Coca-Cola collaboration with Pop Mart. It uses a lighthearted sibling-prank narrative to hook the audience before transitioning into a detailed unboxing that highlights the variety and charm of the collectible plushies.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct showcase of product packaging, close-ups of brand logos, and explicit verbal mention of the collection name.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Fast-paced editing with colorful Chinese text overlays and high-energy female duo commentary.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video leans heavily into 'blind box' culture and the trend of customizing plush toys with accessories.
